comment: Scaffold for V2 Routines Canvas Builder + V1.5 follow-throughs. Canonical spec: docs/proposals/routines-visual-canvas-builder.md. 16 tasks: 13 canvas (P1-P4) + 2 V1.5 + 1 polish. Same per-task body format as the V1 batch.

tasks.title: [V2 P1] Protocol `RoutineLayout` field + JSON schema
tasks.body:
## Phase
P1 - Canvas authoring layer (visual-only, no runtime changes)

## Scope
Add an OPTIONAL `layout` field to `RoutineSpec` carrying canvas node positions and edges. Persists inside the same `spec_yaml` column so no DB migration is needed. V1 routines without `layout` continue to work; canvas mode generates one on first save.

## Approach
- `packages/protocol/src/index.ts`: add `RoutineLayout` type with `version: 1`, `nodes: Record<id, { x: number; y: number; collapsed?: boolean }>`, `edges: Array<{ from: string; to: string; kind?: 'success'|'error'|'true'|'false'|'manual'; label?: string }>`. Add `layout?: RoutineLayout` to `RoutineSpec`.
- `packages/protocol/src/schemas/`: new `routine-layout.json` describing the shape; reference it as optional under `routine-spec.json`'s top-level properties.
- `packages/protocol/src/validate.test.ts`: cover (a) routine without `layout` validates, (b) routine with valid `layout` validates, (c) edge referencing nonexistent node fails with a clear error.
- No runtime changes — the engine ignores `layout` entirely.

## Acceptance
- `bun test` in `packages/protocol` passes.
- A YAML routine with a `layout` block round-trips through `parseSpec`/`stringifySpec` losslessly.
- A routine WITHOUT `layout` still validates (backwards compatible).

tasks.title: [V2 P1] React Flow integration + base canvas mode in RoutineEditor
tasks.body:
## Phase
P1 - Canvas authoring layer

## Scope
Add `@xyflow/react` and stand up a `RoutineCanvas` component that renders an empty canvas with pan/zoom/grid. Add a 'Canvas' tab to the existing `RoutineBuilder` alongside Steps / Triggers / Settings / Spec. New routines default to Canvas; existing routines that have a `layout` block default to Canvas too.

## Approach
- `bun add @xyflow/react` in `apps/web`.
- New files under `apps/web/src/components/routines/canvas/`:
  - `RoutineCanvas.tsx` - top-level canvas shell, mounts React Flow with deck theme tokens (no neon palette - keep IBM Plex / paper-ink colors).
  - `nodes/StepNode.tsx` - custom node component (basic version: type strip + id + one-line summary, no editing yet).
  - `edges/SequentialEdge.tsx` - default curved edge.
  - `graph-types.ts` - internal `CanvasNode` / `CanvasEdge` types derived from `RoutineStep` + `RoutineLayout`.
- Wire into `RoutineBuilder.tsx`: add a 'Canvas' tab in `TabBar`; render `RoutineCanvas` when active.
- React Flow theme overrides: replace default colors with the deck tokens (`--paper-2` background, `--line` strokes, `--accent` for selection). NO bright/neon palette.

## Acceptance
- Opening an existing V1 routine shows the Canvas tab with an empty React Flow surface (since no nodes are wired yet in this task - that's P1.3).
- Theme matches the rest of the deck (no jarring color shift).
- Pan and zoom work via mouse/trackpad.

tasks.title: [V2 P1] Graph import - render existing V1 routines as vertical node graphs
tasks.body:
## Phase
P1 - Canvas authoring layer

## Scope
When opening a V1 routine without a saved `layout`, infer one: lay out steps vertically (220px apart), draw sequential edges between adjacent steps, and add secondary dependency edges for any `when:` expression that references another step's output. Read-only at this stage - editing comes in P2.

## Approach
- New `apps/web/src/components/routines/canvas/graph-import.ts`:
  - `importFromSpec(spec: RoutineSpec): { nodes: CanvasNode[]; edges: CanvasEdge[] }`
  - If `spec.layout` exists, use it verbatim.
  - Otherwise: walk `spec.steps`, place node N at `{ x: 280, y: N * 220 }`, create sequential edges `step[N] -> step[N+1]`.
  - For each step's `when:` string, regex-scan for `steps.<id>.` references; for each unique `<id>` that isn't the immediately-preceding step, add an edge `<id> -> currentStep` with `kind: 'inferred'`.
  - Inferred edges render dashed to distinguish them visually.
- `StepNode.tsx`: render the type strip + id + (for each type) a one-line summary of the most important field (e.g. agent prompt first 60 chars, http url, write path).

## Acceptance
- The shipped `daily-briefing` routine opens as 7 connected nodes in vertical order.
- A step with `when: steps.should_run.json === true` has a dashed inferred edge from `should_run`.
- Node visual is dense but readable - matches the deck's restrained aesthetic, not 'maximalist node editor'.

tasks.title: [V2 P1] Pan/zoom + drag-position persistence into `layout.nodes`
tasks.body:
## Phase
P1 - Canvas authoring layer

## Scope
Make the canvas state persistent. Dragging a node updates the in-memory spec's `layout.nodes[id].x/y`, which serializes back to `spec_yaml` on save. Reopening the routine restores the saved positions.

## Approach
- `RoutineCanvas.tsx`: hook React Flow's `onNodesChange` to push position updates back into the parent `RoutineSpec`.
- `spec-yaml.ts`: extend `stringifySpec` to emit the `layout` block if present, with stable key order (`nodes` before `edges`, sorted by node id).
- `RoutineBuilder.tsx`: thread the canvas-state updates through the same `updateSpec` path used by form mode so YAML round-trip stays consistent.
- Save flow: existing `routinesApi.update` already accepts a `specYaml`; no new endpoint work.

## Acceptance
- Drag the `fetch_tasks` node 100px to the right, click Save, refresh the page - node is at the new position.
- The `layout.nodes.fetch_tasks` block in the saved YAML shows the new coords.
- Form mode and Spec tab still work; switching tabs doesn't lose canvas position changes.

tasks.title: [V2 P2] Toolbar palette - typed node creation
tasks.body:
## Phase
P2 - Node CRUD + property inspector

## Scope
A horizontal toolbar at the top of the canvas with one button per step type. Clicking a button creates a new scaffolded node at the canvas center (or current cursor position), with sensible default field values reusing the existing `scaffoldStep` helper from form mode.

## Approach
- New `apps/web/src/components/routines/canvas/CanvasToolbar.tsx`: horizontal flex of buttons. Layout:
  ```
  [+ run] [+ agent] [+ write] [+ http] [+ deck v] [+ if] [+ wait] [+ transform] [+ set_state] [+ mcp]
  ```
  The deck button is a dropdown (4 actions). The 'if' button creates a `transform` step with a boolean body and visual branch handles (P3 wires the compilation).
- Reuse `scaffoldStep()` from `spec-yaml.ts` for default field values.
- New node placement: center of current viewport via React Flow's `useReactFlow().screenToFlowPosition()`.
- Node ids: derive from type + monotonic counter, dedup against existing ids (same pattern as `pickUniqueId` in `spec-yaml.ts`).

## Acceptance
- Clicking '+ agent' creates a new agent node at canvas center.
- The new node appears in the spec's `steps` array on save.
- Toolbar visually matches the deck's restrained chip style; no neon outlines.

tasks.title: [V2 P2] Property inspector - reuse existing StepForms for selected-node editing
tasks.body:
## Phase
P2 - Node CRUD + property inspector

## Scope
Clicking a node selects it and opens a side panel on the right of the canvas with the existing per-type form (StepForms.tsx) scoped to that single node. Edits flow through the same `replaceStep` path used by form mode.

## Approach
- New `apps/web/src/components/routines/canvas/NodePropertyPanel.tsx`: thin wrapper around `StepCommonFields` + the appropriate `StepForms` form, given a selected `RoutineStep`.
- `RoutineCanvas.tsx`: track selected-node id; render the panel as a 320px right rail inside the canvas viewport when a node is selected.
- Clicking the canvas background deselects; clicking another node swaps the panel.
- Edits dispatch through the parent `onChange` so form mode stays in sync if the user switches tabs.

## Acceptance
- Selecting the `write_to_inbox` node opens the panel pre-filled with its fields.
- Editing the `title` field in the panel updates the canvas node summary in real time.
- Switching to form-mode and back preserves the edit.

tasks.title: [V2 P2] Edge creation/deletion + node deletion via canvas
tasks.body:
## Phase
P2 - Node CRUD + property inspector

## Scope
Drag from a node's source handle to another node's target handle to create an edge. Click an edge + Delete to remove. Click a node + Delete to remove the node (and any edges referencing it). All updates flow back into `spec.layout`.

## Approach
- React Flow's `onConnect` callback adds a new edge to the layout; default `kind: 'success'`.
- `onEdgesDelete` and `onNodesDelete` callbacks remove entries from `layout.edges` and `layout.nodes` (and the underlying `spec.steps` entry).
- Cascade: deleting a node also drops any edge referencing it as `from` or `to`.
- Keyboard: Delete/Backspace on a selected node or edge.
- Confirm dialog on node delete (since it removes a real step from the spec) but not on edge delete (cheap to re-draw).

## Acceptance
- Drag from `fetch_tasks` source to `digest` target creates a sequential edge.
- Deleting a node removes it from both the canvas and the `steps:` YAML on save.
- Confirm dialog prevents accidental node deletion.

tasks.title: [V2 P2] Graph compiler - validate + topo-sort to ordered `steps:`
tasks.body:
## Phase
P2 - Node CRUD + property inspector

## Scope
On save, compile the graph to a valid linear `steps:` array. Validate: unique node IDs, no cycles, no dangling edges, every node reachable from at least one trigger anchor (initial node). Surface errors inline before allowing save.

## Approach
- New `apps/web/src/components/routines/canvas/graph-compile.ts`:
  - `compileGraph(nodes, edges): { steps: RoutineStep[]; errors: CanvasError[] }`
  - Validations (in order): duplicate node IDs, edges referencing missing nodes, cycle detection (DFS with color marking), reachability from canvas root.
  - On success: topological sort (Kahn's algorithm), emit `steps:` in topo order.
- `RoutineCanvas.tsx`: render errors as a bottom strip + red badges on offending nodes. Disable the Save button when errors exist.
- Save flow: existing `RoutineBuilder` save calls `compileGraph()` before serializing, replacing the spec's `steps` array with the compiled output. The `layout` block is also written so canvas reopens identically.

## Acceptance
- A graph with a cycle (A -> B -> A) blocks save with a clear 'cycle detected' error pointing at both nodes.
- A valid graph saves and round-trips: reopen produces the same node positions + same step order.
- Adding a new step in canvas mode, saving, and viewing the Spec tab shows the compiled YAML with the new step in the right position.

tasks.title: [V2 P3] Visual `if` node - true/false handles compiling to `transform` step
tasks.body:
## Phase
P3 - Branching via visual `if` node

## Scope
Add an `if`-flavored node to the toolbar. Visually it's a diamond/rounded-rect with two output handles labeled `true` and `false`. Under the hood it compiles to a `transform` step returning a boolean, plus `when:` gates on downstream nodes consuming each branch.

## Approach
- `CanvasToolbar.tsx`: add the '+ if' button.
- `scaffoldStep('if', ...)` (new helper, NOT a runtime step type): returns a `transform` step with body `return /* condition */ true;` and flags it internally as `__canvasKind: 'if'` (carried in the canvas-local state, NOT serialized into the spec).
- `StepNode.tsx`: when the node is marked `__canvasKind: 'if'`, render two output handles instead of one: `true` (top) and `false` (bottom). Render the body's source as the node summary.
- `NodePropertyPanel.tsx`: when an `if` node is selected, show a simplified editor - just the condition expression - instead of the full `transform` body.
- The runtime engine never sees `__canvasKind`; it only sees the compiled `transform` step.

## Acceptance
- Adding an `if` node and connecting one downstream node to its `true` handle and another to its `false` handle works in the canvas.
- The compiled spec contains a `transform` step with the boolean body.
- Saving and reopening preserves the canvas-side `if` shape (via the `layout.edges[].kind` field set to `'true'`/`'false'`).

tasks.title: [V2 P3] Branch-edge compilation - AND-merge `when:` onto downstream nodes
tasks.body:
## Phase
P3 - Branching via visual `if` node

## Scope
During graph compilation, edges leaving an `if` node with `kind: 'true'`/`'false'` get compiled into `when:` expressions on the downstream node. If the downstream node already has a manually-authored `when:`, AND-merge so both conditions hold.

## Approach
- Extend `compileGraph()` in `graph-compile.ts`:
  - For each edge whose source is an `if` node, derive `branchWhen` from the edge kind:
    - `kind: 'true'` -> `steps.<sourceId>.json === true`
    - `kind: 'false'` -> `steps.<sourceId>.json === false`
  - Apply to the target node's `when` field by AND-merging with the existing expression: `(existing) && (branchWhen)` (with parens to avoid precedence bugs).
  - If the target has no existing `when`, set it directly to `branchWhen`.
  - Multiple branch edges into the same target (rare but possible) are AND-merged together.
- Add unit tests in `packages/protocol/src/` (or a new server-side test) covering:
  - Single-branch compile: `true` edge produces `when: steps.X.json === true`.
  - AND-merge with existing `when` preserves the original condition.
  - Two branch edges into one node compose correctly.

## Acceptance
- A graph with `if(should_send) -> agent_send` (true branch) compiles `agent_send` to `when: steps.should_send.json === true`.
- If `agent_send` already had `when: state.enabled !== false`, the compiled output is `when: (state.enabled !== false) && (steps.should_send.json === true)`.
- Unit tests cover both cases.

tasks.title: [V2 P3] Pre-save diff preview - show generated YAML before commit
tasks.body:
## Phase
P3 - Branching via visual `if` node

## Scope
Before the user clicks Save from canvas mode, show them the YAML that will be written, with a visual diff against the current saved spec. Critical because branch compilation is non-obvious; the user should see exactly what's getting persisted.

## Approach
- New `apps/web/src/components/routines/canvas/SavePreviewDialog.tsx`: modal that opens when the user clicks Save from canvas mode (if it's a new save or the YAML differs).
- Render the compiled YAML in a `<pre>` with subtle diff styling: green-tinted lines for additions, red-tinted for removals, vs the currently-saved spec. Use a simple line-diff (no need for a full diff lib - a basic LCS implementation in a helper is fine).
- Buttons: 'Save' (commits), 'Edit canvas' (cancels, returns to canvas).
- Skip the preview if `OMP_DECK_CANVAS_SKIP_PREVIEW=1` is set (power-user opt-out).

## Acceptance
- Drawing an `if` branch and clicking Save shows the dialog with the compiled `when: steps.X.json === true` line highlighted as an addition.
- Cancelling returns to the canvas with no changes.
- 'Save' commits via the existing `routinesApi.update` and closes the dialog.

tasks.title: [V2 P4] Run-status overlay on nodes
tasks.body:
## Phase
P4 - Run overlay (observability fusion)

## Scope
After a routine runs, paint each canvas node with the status of its corresponding step from the most-recent run: success (green ring), failed (red ring), skipped (gray ring), aborted (warn-color ring), running (accent ring with pulse). Show duration, model, and cost badges where relevant.

## Approach
- `RoutineCanvas.tsx`: fetch the most-recent run's step list via `routinesApi.steps(routineId, latestRunId)` on mount and on WS frame `routine_step_event` / `routine_run_finished`.
- `StepNode.tsx`: accept an optional `stepRun?: RoutineStepRun` prop; render the ring color + status badge + duration (`X.Xs`) + (for agent steps) model name + cost (`$0.012`).
- Live updates: subscribe to `routine_step_event` frames so a node's ring color changes in real time during a run.
- Toolbar gets a 'last run' selector if there are multiple runs in the last 30, so the user can scrub through history.

## Acceptance
- Running `daily-briefing` from the canvas paints each node's ring as it completes.
- Skipped steps (e.g. when `should_run` returns false) show with the skipped style and remain dim.
- The last-run selector switches the overlay to a prior run.

tasks.title: [V2 P4] Node click -> inline output preview + replay-from-here link
tasks.body:
## Phase
P4 - Run overlay (observability fusion)

## Scope
When viewing a node with a recent run, clicking opens a small popover (or extends the property panel) showing the captured output: stdout excerpt, stderr, structured JSON, error. Plus a 'Replay from here' link that opens RunDetailView jumped to that step.

## Approach
- Extend `NodePropertyPanel.tsx`: when a `stepRun` is available for the selected node, show a 'Last run' section below the form with:
  - status pill
  - duration
  - tabs for stdout / stderr / json / error (whichever are non-empty)
  - 'Open in Run Detail' link to `/routines/:id/runs/:runId#step-:stepId`
- `RunDetailView`: support a hash fragment that scrolls to + expands the named step.

## Acceptance
- After a daily-briefing run, clicking the `digest` node shows its structured JSON output inline.
- 'Open in Run Detail' navigates to the right URL and the targeted step is expanded on load.
- Closing the canvas and reopening preserves the most-recent run overlay until a newer run replaces it.

tasks.title: [V1.5] Workspace MCP install path + inbox-triager template
tasks.body:
## Phase
V1.5 - second proof point

## Scope
Ship the inbox-triager routine as the second canonical template, exercising the V1.5 Workspace MCP integration end-to-end. Reuses the deferred work from the V1 plan's Phase 4-5.

## Approach
- `apps/server/src/templates/inbox-triager.yaml`: per the V1 plan's section 4.4 reference spec. Triggers: cron */15 9-19 * * 1-5 + manual webhook. Steps: `mcp` fetch unread, `agent` classify, `agent` apply actions with `mcp_servers_allowed: ['google-workspace']`, `set_state` persist cursor, `deck` action `create_inbox_item` for a daily summary.
- Integrations page (`/integrations`): replace the V1.5 stub with the install flow:
  - 'Connect Google Workspace' button -> OAuth consent flow (Google client id from env).
  - Persists refresh token to deck `.env` via the existing settings env API.
  - Registers the shared MCP sidecar URL in `~/.omp/agent/mcp.json`.
- Smoke: install via `POST /api/routine-templates/inbox-triager`, fire manually, verify it creates an inbox capture summarizing what was triaged.

## Acceptance
- The Integrations page walks a user from zero to a connected Google Workspace account.
- `inbox-triager` template installs and validates against the schema.
- A manual run end-to-end completes and creates a deck inbox item.
- Acceptance also requires T-74 (mcp step unstub) to be merged first.

tasks.title: [V1.5] Real `mcp` step type - unstub via SDK MCP client
tasks.body:
## Phase
V1.5 - engine completion

## Scope
The `mcp` step type is currently a stub that returns a clear V1.5 pointer. Wire it to the SDK's MCP client surface so it can actually invoke tools on installed servers (via stdio or streamable-http transport).

## Approach
- Audit the omp SDK for an MCP-tool-call surface. The deck's bridge already loads MCP servers via `loadCapability`; the question is whether there's a direct `callMcpTool(serverName, toolName, args)` API or whether the deck must spawn one through the agent.
- If direct API exists: implement `executeMcpStep` to look up the server + tool, invoke with templated args, capture the result as `steps.X.json`.
- If no direct API yet: file an SDK-bridge feature request (`pi-coding-agent` repo) for one, then implement against that.
- Update `step-mcp.json` schema if any new optional fields fall out of implementation (e.g. timeout-per-call).
- Add server-side tests against a fake MCP server fixture.

## Acceptance
- A routine with `type: mcp; server: filesystem; tool: read_text_file; args: { path: '...' }` actually reads the file via the MCP server and captures the result.
- Errors (server not registered, tool not found, bad args) produce a clear `error` field on the step run.
- Existing routines that don't use `mcp` are unaffected.

tasks.title: [Polish] Finish T-57 release docs - README routines pitch + screenshot
tasks.body:
## Phase
Polish - finish T-57 properly

## Scope
T-57 (V1 release docs) was marked done in the deck but only partially landed: CHANGELOG entry + tutorial shipped, but README still has the V0 routines copy and there is no shipped routines screenshot under `docs/screenshots/`. Close the gap.

## Approach
- Update `README.md`:
  - Replace the V0 routines bullet ('Cron scheduler...') with a V1 pitch: multi-step pipelines, visual builder, deck-native action steps, run observability, daily-briefing template.
  - Add a short snippet of the daily-briefing YAML or a screenshot of the builder.
- `docs/screenshots/routines-builder.png`: capture a clean shot of the routines builder showing daily-briefing's 7 steps in canvas mode (once V2 P1-P3 are in) or form mode (if pushing this before canvas ships).
- Update `docs/install.md` if it currently references the V0 single-action model.

## Acceptance
- `README.md` mentions V1 routines + visual builder.
- A screenshot exists under `docs/screenshots/` and is referenced from the README.
- A first-time reader of the README understands what routines are without opening the tutorial.
